# Foundations: Why Image Optimisation Matters & How Browsers Load Images

As a **Senior React/Frontend Engineer**, understanding image optimisation is essential because images are often the **largest contributor to page weight** and can significantly impact load times, Core Web Vitals, SEO, and user experience. Some performance guides note that images frequently account for a large share of total page size on modern websites. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/hardik_b2d8f0bca/how-to-optimize-images-for-faster-loading-a-developers-complete-guide-e8b), [\[web.dev\]](https://web.dev/articles/browser-level-image-lazy-loading)

***

# Why Image Optimisation Matters

## Without Optimisation

```text
HTML = 50 KB
CSS  = 100 KB
JS   = 300 KB
Images = 5 MB

Total:
~5.45 MB
```

Browser spends most of its time downloading images.

Problems:

```text
❌ Slow page load
❌ Higher bandwidth usage
❌ Poor mobile experience
❌ Worse Core Web Vitals
❌ Lower SEO performance
```

Image optimisation can reduce transferred data, improve loading metrics, and lower bandwidth consumption. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/hardik_b2d8f0bca/how-to-optimize-images-for-faster-loading-a-developers-complete-guide-e8b), [\[aleksandrh...nisyan.com\]](https://www.aleksandrhovhannisyan.com/blog/optimizing-images-for-the-web/)

***

# How Browsers Load Images

When the browser receives:

&lt;img&nbsp; src="hero.jpg"&nbsp; alt="Hero"/&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

It roughly performs:

```text
1. Download HTML
        ↓
2. Parse HTML
        ↓
3. Build DOM
        ↓
4. Discover image URL
        ↓
5. Request image
        ↓
6. Download image
        ↓
7. Decode image
        ↓
8. Paint image
```

Images require both network transfer and decoding before they can be rendered. [\[developer....ozilla.org\]](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Performance/Guides/Lazy_loading), [\[web.dev\]](https://web.dev/articles/browser-level-image-lazy-loading)

***

# Critical Rendering Path

```text
HTML
  ↓
DOM
  ↓
Layout
  ↓
Paint
```

Images can affect:

```text
LCP (Largest Contentful Paint)

CLS (Layout Shift)

INP (Interaction)
```

That is why image optimisation has a direct impact on perceived page performance and Core Web Vitals. [\[imgfast.com\]](https://imgfast.com/learn/optimization/lazy-loading), [\[blog.mikihands.com\]](https://blog.mikihands.com/en/whitedec/2025/7/14/lazy-loading-web-performance-guide/)

***

# Choose the Right Format

## JPEG

Best for:

```text
Photos
Product Images
Banners
```

Pros:

```text
✅ Small size
✅ Good compatibility
```

***

## PNG

Best for:

```text
Logos
Transparent Images
Screenshots
```

Pros:

```text
✅ Lossless
✅ Transparency
```

Cons:

```text
❌ Larger file size
```

***

## WebP

Pros:

```text
✅ Smaller than JPEG
✅ Supports transparency
✅ Broad browser support
```

Many guides describe WebP as providing substantially smaller files than equivalent JPEG/PNG images while maintaining comparable quality. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/hardik_b2d8f0bca/how-to-optimize-images-for-faster-loading-a-developers-complete-guide-e8b), [\[aleksandrh...nisyan.com\]](https://www.aleksandrhovhannisyan.com/blog/optimizing-images-for-the-web/)

***

## AVIF

Pros:

```text
✅ Even smaller files
✅ Modern format
```

Often used together with fallbacks for broader compatibility. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/hardik_b2d8f0bca/how-to-optimize-images-for-faster-loading-a-developers-complete-guide-e8b)

***

# Responsive Images

Bad:

&lt;img src="4000px.jpg" /&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Phone only needs:

```text
400px
```

Yet downloads:

```text
4000px image
```

***

Good:

&lt;img&nbsp; srcset="&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; small.jpg 400w,&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; medium.jpg 800w,&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; large.jpg 1600w&nbsp; "&nbsp; sizes="&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; (max-width:600px) 100vw,&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; 50vw&nbsp; "&nbsp; src="large.jpg"/&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Responsive image techniques allow browsers to choose the most appropriate image size for the device. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/hardik_b2d8f0bca/how-to-optimize-images-for-faster-loading-a-developers-complete-guide-e8b)

***

# Lazy Loading

## Problem

Page contains:

```text
50 images
```

User sees:

```text
Only first 3
```

Yet browser downloads all 50.

***

## Solution

&lt;img jpg![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Browser delays loading until the image approaches the viewport. Native browser lazy loading is now broadly supported and reduces unnecessary downloads for off-screen content. [\[web.dev\]](https://web.dev/articles/browser-level-image-lazy-loading), [\[imgfast.com\]](https://imgfast.com/learn/optimization/lazy-loading), [\[developer....ozilla.org\]](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Performance/Guides/Lazy_loading)

### Important

Do **not** lazy-load:

```text
Hero image
Above-the-fold image
LCP image
```

Guidance from performance resources explicitly warns against lazy-loading images likely to appear in the first viewport. [\[web.dev\]](https://web.dev/articles/browser-level-image-lazy-loading), [\[imgfast.com\]](https://imgfast.com/learn/optimization/lazy-loading)

***

# Specify Dimensions

Bad:

hero.jpg![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Can cause:

```text
Layout Shift (CLS)
```

***

Good:

hero.jpg![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

This helps reserve layout space before the image loads. [\[blog.mikihands.com\]](https://blog.mikihands.com/en/whitedec/2025/7/14/lazy-loading-web-performance-guide/), [\[imgfast.com\]](https://imgfast.com/learn/optimization/lazy-loading)

***

# Image Loading Priorities

## Critical Images

hero.jpg![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

***

## Non-Critical

gallery.jpg![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Browsers prioritise images differently based on location relative to the viewport. [\[web.dev\]](https://web.dev/articles/browser-level-image-lazy-loading)

***

# React Example

```jsx
function ProductCard({
  product,
}) {
  return (
    {product.image}
  );
}
```

***

# Next.js Optimised Approach

```jsx
import Image from "next/image";

/product.jpg
```

Benefits:

```text
✅ Responsive images
✅ Lazy loading
✅ Optimised formats
✅ Automatic sizing
```

***

# What to Check in Chrome DevTools

### Network Tab

```text
Image Size
Image Request Count
Compression
```

### Lighthouse

```text
Serve images in next-gen formats
Properly size images
Offscreen image loading
```

These are common image-related recommendations surfaced by web performance auditing tools. [\[aleksandrh...nisyan.com\]](https://www.aleksandrhovhannisyan.com/blog/optimizing-images-for-the-web/), [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/hardik_b2d8f0bca/how-to-optimize-images-for-faster-loading-a-developers-complete-guide-e8b)

***

# Senior Frontend Interview Answer

> Browsers discover image resources while parsing HTML, then download, decode, and paint them. Because images typically consume a large portion of page bandwidth, optimisation is critical. I optimise images by selecting modern formats such as WebP/AVIF where appropriate, serving responsive sizes through `srcset`, specifying dimensions to avoid layout shifts, and lazy-loading only non-critical off-screen images. This improves loading performance, bandwidth usage, and Core Web Vitals. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/hardik_b2d8f0bca/how-to-optimize-images-for-faster-loading-a-developers-complete-guide-e8b), [\[aleksandrh...nisyan.com\]](https://www.aleksandrhovhannisyan.com/blog/optimizing-images-for-the-web/), [\[web.dev\]](https://web.dev/articles/browser-level-image-lazy-loading)
